source.title: Varicose veins: diagnosis and management — Recommendations
source.jurisdiction: England and Wales; international evidence requiring UAE adaptation
source.population: Adults with varicose veins, lower-limb symptoms, skin changes, venous ulceration, or venous insufficiency context.
source.setting: Primary care and vascular-service recognition and referral assessment.
source.exclusions: Exact for lower-limb pain, aching, discomfort, swelling, heaviness, itching, pigmentation, eczema, and chronic-venous-insufficiency context. It does not diagnose stasis dermatitis or venous eczema and does not generate referral or treatment. This batch additionally reviews adult symptom, complication, referral, duplex and pregnancy recommendations while preserving the previously registered chronic-venous-skin and ulcer-history sections. rewrites.final_wording: Supports documenting lower-limb pain, aching, discomfort, swelling, heaviness, itching, pigmentation, and eczema considered by the clinician in chronic venous insufficiency context.

source.title: Varicose veins: diagnosis and management — Recommendations
source.jurisdiction: England and Wales; international evidence requiring UAE adaptation
source.population: Adults with varicose veins, lower-limb symptoms, skin changes, venous ulceration, or venous insufficiency context.
source.setting: Primary care and vascular-service recognition and referral assessment.
source.exclusions: Exact for lower-limb pain, aching, discomfort, swelling, heaviness, itching, pigmentation, eczema, and chronic-venous-insufficiency context. It does not diagnose stasis dermatitis or venous eczema and does not generate referral or treatment. This batch additionally reviews adult symptom, complication, referral, duplex and pregnancy recommendations while preserving the previously registered chronic-venous-skin and ulcer-history sections. No diagnosis, imaging or treatment is generated. curation_policy: Direct full-source guideline reconstruction; every legacy item has an auditable comparison and every final clinical item retains exact source provenance.
